title: 10 best herbal creams for jaw clenching

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream helps to relax the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw, reducing spasms and tension that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching.

Muscle-relaxing properties: The essential oils in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have a sedative effect on muscle tissue, helping to ease muscle tension and pain associated with jaw clenching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its ability to calm the mind and reduce muscle tension. The herb contains compounds that have a natural relaxing effect, which can help alleviate the stress and anxiety that often contribute to jaw clenching. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, Melissa officinalis creams can help ease the physical symptoms of jaw clenching, such as facial pain and tension headaches. This can lead to improved overall well-being and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural relaxant, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce stress-related muscle contractions that contribute to jaw clenching.
Glycosides: These compounds, particularly verbascoside, have been found to possess mild anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help to mitigate stress-induced jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe jaw clenching and related muscle tension.
Stems: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Roots: They are used due to their sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which help to calm the mind and body, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the plant is in optimal condition.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a large.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean glass container to separate.
Mix the reserved liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant properties: Maypop cream may help relax tense muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing discomfort and pain associated with jaw clenching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream could potentially reduce inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, alleviating pressure and pain that contributes to jaw clenching.
Calming and sedative effects: Maypop has been traditionally used as a natural relaxant, which may help calm nervous energy and promote relaxation, reducing the urge to grind or clench one's teeth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with jaw clenching because its active compounds, flavonoids and alkaloids, work to relax the muscles and reduce anxiety. The sedative properties of Passiflora incarnata help to calm the nervous system, alleviating stress and tension that can contribute to jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, Passiflora incarnata creams provide a natural remedy to soothe jaw tension and promote a sense of calm and well-being. This can lead to a significant reduction in jaw clenching and associated discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hernandezioside: Hernandezioside is an iridoid glycoside that helps with jaw clenching by exerting a relaxing effect on the muscles, reducing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation.
Passiflorine: Passiflorine is an alkaloid that helps with jaw clenching by acting as a central nervous system depressant, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw clenching.
Isoorientin: Isoorientin is a flavonoid that helps with jaw clenching by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties that help reduce muscle tension.
Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of harman alkaloids, which have sedative and muscle relaxant effects, making them effective in calming jaw clenching.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain passifloric acid and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties, helping to soothe jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Steep 20 grams of dried passiflora incarnata in 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture and discard the solids before blending it with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and stable cream base.

remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian root's sedative compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, help relax tense muscles in the face and jaw, reducing symptoms of bruxism (jaw clenching) by calming muscle spasms.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation and swelling associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which often accompany jaw clenching, and alleviate pain and discomfort in the face and jaw.
Anxiolytic Properties: Valerian root's calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for jaw clenching, by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension in the face and jaw.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its natural muscle-relaxing properties. The active compounds in valerian, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension. This leads to a decrease in the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes. Additionally, valerian creams can also promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. By addressing the underlying causes of jaw clenching, valerian creams provide a natural and effective solution for relief.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with jaw clenching by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation.
Valeranone: It aids in reducing jaw clenching by having sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to clenching.
Isovaleramide: It contributes to relieving jaw clenching by exhibiting a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect and helps to relax muscles.
Leaves: They are used as they contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to their calming properties and ability to soothe muscle tension.
Barks: They are used due to their content of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and reduce muscle spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 100g of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 100g of the powdered root with 100g of beeswax and 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Blend the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes then remove from heat to cool and thicken slowly.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The active compound cinnamaldehyde in Ceylon cinnamon has anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, including jaw clenching.

Antioxidant property: Cinnamon's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and inflammation in the muscles of the face and jaw, contributing to jaw clenching.

Muscle relaxant property: The essential oils present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid, have a natural relaxing effect on muscles, which can help to ease tension in the jaw and facial muscles that contributes to jaw clenching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde and linalool, work to reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles. This can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. Regular application of Ceylon cinnamon cream may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, contributing to a decrease in jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles, making it easier to relax and reduce clenching.

Cinnamic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Linalool: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw clenching, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the jaw area.
Leaves: They are used due to their calming and soothing effects, which help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Rhyzomes: They are used due to their ability to reduce pain and inflammation, which helps to alleviate jaw clenching and tension.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of cinnamomum verum bark in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 4-6 weeks to extract essential properties.
Filter the infused oil using a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids and achieve a clear consistency.
Weigh out 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of cocoa butter to create a stable emollient base.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax and cocoa butter mixture in a double boiler and heat until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw tension, making it easier to relax and release clenched muscles.
Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin and mucous membranes, which can help to numb the area and temporarily relieve muscle spasms and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

Vitamin E: Vitamin E is an antioxidant that helps to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, which may contribute to jaw tension and clenching; it also promotes healthy skin and mucous membranes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint creams relaxes tense muscles, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to soothe and calm the jaw and facial areas, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency of clenching episodes. This natural remedy provides relief from tension headaches and jaw pain caused by teeth grinding or clenching.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This monoterpene has been shown to have a relaxing effect on muscles, which may help to reduce the tension associated with jaw clenching.
Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, rosmarinic acid may help to alleviate pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
Menthofuran: A sesquiterpene with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, menthofuran may help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide pain-relieving and cooling properties.
Stems: They contain a smaller amount of menthol and menthone compared to leaves, but their essential oils still contribute to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects in creams.
Roots: They are sometimes used as a substitute for leaves when the latter are not available, and their essential oils also possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in late morning when their essential oil content is at its highest peak.
Infuse 250g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 1l of distilled water for 2-3 hours to release their active compounds.
Strain the infused liquid using a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a fragrant herbal extract.
Mix 50g of beeswax, 100g of coconut oil, and 20ml of the herbal extract in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Pour the melted mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and protect the muscles and tissues of the jaw from oxidative stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle Relaxation Compounds: Lavender contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on muscle tissue, helping to relax the jaw muscles and reduce tension.

Sedative Properties: The sedative properties of lavender help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common causes of jaw clenching, by calming the nervous system and promoting a sense of calm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The scent of lavender has been shown to reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals who suffer from jaw clenching and related stress and anxiety. The cream's soothing effects can help to calm the mind and body, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce muscle tension and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester that promotes relaxation, reduces anxiety, and calms the nervous system, thereby alleviating jaw clenching caused by stress and muscle spasms.
Lavandulol: A terpene with anxiolytic and sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia's leaves are used to make creams for jaw clenching due to their calming and antispasmodic properties, which help relax muscles and reduce tension.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia's flowers are used to make creams for jaw clenching due to their soothing and sedative effects, which promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia's stems are used to make creams for jaw clenching due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula flowers and 2 tablespoons of beeswax at dawn when plant's oils are most potent.
Infuse 1 cup of dried lavandula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 4 weeks stirring daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard solids then mix with 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized jar and let it cool and thicken for 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles and joints caused by clenching.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the jaw tissues from damage and reduce inflammation caused by repeated clenching and grinding.

Licorice saponins: These compounds have a relaxing effect on muscle tissue, which can help reduce tension in the jaw muscles and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as jaw pain and clicking.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root extract of Glycyrrhiza glabra contains compounds that relax muscles and reduce stress, making it an effective remedy for jaw tension and bruxism. The cream's ability to calm the jaw and surrounding facial muscles also helps to prevent the buildup of stress and anxiety that can lead to jaw clenching, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These saponins help alleviate jaw clenching by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the face and jaw area, thereby providing relief from tension headaches and jaw pain.
Glycyrrhetinic Acid: As a potent anti-inflammatory agent, glycyrrhetinic acid helps reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and facial muscles, making it easier to relax and reduce jaw clenching.

Flavonoids (specifically, Isoliquiritigenin): These flavonoids possess anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which help calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension in the jaw, thereby reducing jaw clenching and associated pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the roots, helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, alleviating jaw clenching symptoms.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin also has anti-anxiety properties, which can help reduce stress that may contribute to jaw clenching.
Roots: The licorice root's ability to inhibit the production of stress hormones, such as cortisol, can further help alleviate jaw clenching caused by anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 20 grams of beeswax and infuse the roots in 200 milliliters of oil.
Steep the root infusion in oil mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to release active compounds.
Melt 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to enhance stability.
Combine the cooled root infusion and melted beeswax mixture and stir well to create a uniform texture.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify completely within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Soothes muscle tension: Stevia cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce muscle strain and relax the masseter muscles in the jaw, reducing clenching and grinding.
Reduces pain and discomfort: The analgesic properties of stevia help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with TMJ disorders, such as jaw pain, headaches, and facial tension.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Stevia cream's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help to protect the oral cavity from infection and inflammation, which can contribute to jaw clenching and grinding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia rebaudiana, also known as stevia, creams helps with jaw clenching because it contains compounds that promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of Stevia rebaudiana soothe and calm the jaw muscles, helping to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, Stevia rebaudiana creams can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Steviol Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-anxiety and stress-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of jaw clenching.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Stevia rebaudiana, such as gallic acid and chlorogenic acid, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Stevia rebaudiana, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Stevia rebaudiana due to their high content of steviosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle tension and jaw clenching.

Roots: Roots of Stevia rebaudiana contain a high concentration of glycosides, which have been found to have anti-stress and anti-anxiety effects, potentially alleviating jaw clenching caused by stress and anxiety.
Barks: Stevia rebaudiana barks have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety and stress-related muscle tension, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of stevia rebaudiana leaves in 2 cups of distilled water for 4 to 6 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of glycerin with 2 tablespoons of distilled water and add to the infused liquid to create a stable emulsion.
Whip 8 ounces of shea or cocoa butter with 2 ounces of the infused liquid until a smooth cream forms.
Pour the stevia rebaudiana cream into a clean container and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosin and saponins, which help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles, alleviating tension and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the jaw muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing the risk of chronic pain.

Muscle relaxants: Kansas coneflower cream contains compounds like salicin, which has natural muscle-relaxing properties that help soothe and calm tense jaw muscles, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching and related conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes, work to reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation in the jaw and surrounding areas. This can lead to a decrease in jaw clenching and grinding, providing relief from associated symptoms like headaches and earaches. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia's soothing effects may help alleviate stress and anxiety, further contributing to its ability to alleviate jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw clenching by modulating the body's response to stress and anxiety.
Caffeic Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the muscle tension and pain caused by jaw clenching.
Alkaloids (specifically, Cynoglossoside): These compounds can help relax muscles and reduce stress, making it easier to manage jaw clenching and associated symptoms like tension headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Extracts from the roots are used to make creams for jaw clenching due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Compounds found in the leaves help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks of Echinacea angustifolia contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for creams targeting jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall after the first frost when plants are dormant for 2-3 years.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Infuse 30 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root in 250 milliliters of oil at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and other cream ingredients in a 1:1 ratio by weight and heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw clenching and bruxism, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable chewing motion.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream, including vitamins A, C, and E, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and tissue damage, thereby reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting overall oral health.

Soothing and calming effects: Pot marigold's natural soothing and calming properties, attributed to its flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, can help relax tense jaw muscles and reduce anxiety-related habits like teeth grinding and clenching, providing relief from symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles, providing relief from tension headaches and TMJ disorders. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, Calendula officinalis cream helps to relax the jaw muscles, allowing the jaw to release its clenched position and promoting overall relaxation and well-being. This natural remedy offers a non-invasive solution for jaw clenching relief.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce tension and inflammation in the jaw muscles that contribute to jaw clenching.
Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): As potent antioxidants, flavonoids like Quercetin can help alleviate muscle spasms and reduce the severity of jaw clenching by protecting against oxidative stress.

Naphthoquinone derivatives (especially Plumbagin): These compounds exhibit anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relax the jaw muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area.
Stems: The stems are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the jaw and reduce tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the morning to ensure maximum potency for the herbal cream.
Clean and dry the flowers to prevent contamination and spoilage by air drying them for 2 weeks.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the flowers to infuse their properties into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and mix it with a moisturizing base like beeswax to create a soothing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain a blend of soothing herbs that calm the muscles in the jaw, face, and neck. The warmth from the tea seeps into the skin, relaxing tense areas and reducing discomfort. Herbs like lavender and chamomile have natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain, while others like peppermint and ginger stimulate blood flow to promote relaxation and relieve tension. Regular use of these creams can help alleviate jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Chamomile: Chamomile tea cream has a calming effect on the muscles, which helps to relax the jaw and reduce tension caused by clenching.

Lavender: Lavender tea cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the joints and tissues of the jaw, reducing pain and discomfort associated with bruxism (jaw clenching).

Ginger: Ginger tea cream has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw and surrounding areas, making it a natural remedy for relieving tension headaches caused by jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the jaw muscles.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and tannins that have astringent and anti-spasmodic effects, making them effective in reducing muscle tension and relaxing the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and dry them in the shade for 7 days or more.
Blend dried camellia sinensis leaves with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a 3:2:1 ratio.
Steam distill the blended mixture at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Mix the extracted essential oils with coconut oil and aloe vera gel in a 2:1:1 ratio and store in glass jars.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 24 hours before using as a topical cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with jaw clenching because of its unique blend of soothing and calming properties. The herbal ingredients, particularly damask rose essential oil, work to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, reducing tension and strain caused by teeth grinding and clenching. As the cream is applied to the skin, it releases a gentle, calming energy that penetrates deep into the muscles, easing discomfort and promoting relaxation, ultimately helping to alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain caused by jaw clenching, allowing for smoother movement and relief from tension.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic property of damask rose cream relaxes muscle contractions in the face and jaw, easing the spasms that contribute to jaw clenching and releasing tension.
Soothing and calming property: Damask rose cream's soothing and calming properties help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching, by promoting relaxation and a sense of calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant are used to make medicinal creams because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw.
Seeds: Damask rose seeds are used to make creams as they contain antioxidants that help soothe and calm irritated muscles, reducing tension and discomfort in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosa centifolia petals and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered rose petals with 50ml of jojoba oil and 20ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Stir in 5g of beeswax and 2g of vitamin e oil into the oil mixture, dissolving completely before removing from heat after 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 45 minutes, whipping occasionally with a spatula until smooth consistency is achieved.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with jaw clenching because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a natural compound that reduces muscle tension and relaxes the facial muscles, including those responsible for jaw clenching. When applied topically in cream form, St john's wort helps to soothe and calm the jaw joint, reducing inflammation and pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJD) and bruxism.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Hypotensive effect: St John's Wort has been found to dilate blood vessels and lower blood pressure, which may help alleviate tension-related symptoms such as jaw clenching.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in St John's Wort creams can help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and damage, potentially reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw.
Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective for soothing jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 50 milliliters of coconut oil and 30 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the mixture in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to sterilized jars with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that work in harmony to relax the facial muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel help to reduce tension and stiffness in the jaw, while its calming effects soothe any irritability or anxiety that may be contributing to the clenching. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to hydrate the skin around the jawline, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compound, anethole, helps reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Muscle relaxant property: Fennel contains flavonoids that have a relaxing effect on muscles, helping to ease tension in the jaw, face, and neck, thereby reducing clenching and grinding.

Carminative property: Fennel's essential oils have a carminative effect, which helps expel trapped air and gas from the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating that can exacerbate jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are commonly used in traditional medicine due to their ability to relax muscles and ease tension, making them an effective ingredient in creams for jaw clenching relief.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw clenching, making them a popular choice for topical creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in quantities of 20-30 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base.
Combine the dried seeds with 200 milliliters of olive oil and heat the mixture at a low temperature of 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solid particles and discard the solids.
Mix the filtered oil with 100 milliliters of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture at a temperature of 70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to air-tight containers for storage or application as needed within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint creams helps with jaw clenching because of its calming and relaxing properties. The menthol in spearmint oil helps to numb the affected area, reducing muscle tension and spasms that can lead to jaw clenching. Additionally, the cooling sensation of the cream can help to distract from any pain or discomfort caused by bruxism, promoting relaxation and ease in the jaw muscles. As a result, regular use of spearmint creams can help to alleviate jaw clenching, promoting better oral health and reducing the risk of related issues such as headaches and migraines.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling sensation helps to relax tense muscles, reducing jaw clenching and pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in spearmint, such as menthone and limonene, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with jaw tension and clenching.

Antioxidants: Spearmint's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the mouth and jaw from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the likelihood of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Spearmint roots contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to relax tense muscles and reduce pain associated with jaw clenching.
Rhyzomes: Spearmint rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling in the jaw area, alleviating tension and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha spicata leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or air drying for 72 hours.
Combine 20g of dried mentha spicata with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 10ml of beeswax and stir until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before storing in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe and relax the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles. The cream's active ingredients, such as boswellia and camphor, penetrate deep into the tissues to reduce swelling and pain caused by excessive grinding or clenching of the jaw. As a result, tension is released, and the jaw can move freely without discomfort, leading to improved overall oral function and reduced facial pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which is often caused by jaw clenching.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in frankincense cream can help protect the muscles and joints of the face and jaw from oxidative stress and damage caused by clenching and grinding, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Pain-relieving properties: Frankincense's natural analgesic properties can help numb the area and reduce pain associated with jaw clenching, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed jaw.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.
Barks: The bark of the frankincense plant contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to relax muscle contractions and ease tension in the jaw area.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil into a double boiler for melting.
Combine 10 grams of dried boswellia serrata extract and 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Gradually add the boswellia serrata mixture to the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring until well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer.
Transfer the creamy mixture to sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with jaw clenching because of their unique ability to relax tense muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties found in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to soothe and calm the masseter muscle, which is responsible for closing the jaw. This natural relief can help reduce tension and alleviate symptoms associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, migraines, and even stress-related jaw clenching. Regular use of elder creams can promote a sense of relaxation and ease, leading to a healthier and more comfortable jaw.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder flowers contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding tissues, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in elder creams, such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and polyphenols, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and muscle tension, which can lead to jaw clenching and bruxism.

Calming and soothing agents: Elder flowers and leaves contain compounds like valerenic acid, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the muscles of mastication (chewing) and reduce spasms associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area, making them effective for soothing jaw clenching.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the elder plant have a calming effect on the muscles, helping to relax the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and alleviate tension caused by jaw clenching.
Roots: Elder roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area, making them effective for soothing jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant have analgesic properties that help numb the pain caused by jaw clenching, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers in their prime blooming season for optimal potency.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 8 hours.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra flowers with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 6-8 hours under gentle heat to facilitate herbal extraction.
Strain and filter the resulting cream through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with jaw clenching because it targets the underlying tension and stress that often leads to teeth grinding. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of yarrow extract work to relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing stiffness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation can help calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of relaxation and ease. By addressing the root causes of tension, herbal yarrow creams provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating jaw clenching and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles, relieving tension caused by clenching and grinding.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of yarrow can help relax and soothe the jaw muscles, reducing muscle spasms and contractions that contribute to clenching and grinding.
Antioxidant: Yarrow's antioxidant properties can help protect the jaw tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic tension and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in soothing and calming irritated muscles and joints, making them ideal for relieving tension in the jaw.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids and phytochemicals, seeds help to reduce inflammation and pain, making them suitable for treating jaw clenching and related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested herbs into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle, releasing their oils and essences.
Combine the chopped herbs with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler, allowing the mixture to simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature over several hours before whipping it into a creamy consistency using a hand mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jaw clenching because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion root and chamomile, work together to reduce swelling in the jaw muscles and relax tense facial tissues, thereby alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and bruxism. This allows for smoother, more relaxed jaw movement, reducing clenching and grinding habits that can lead to discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce swelling and pain in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which can contribute to jaw clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids and triterpenes present in dandelion cream have been shown to relax muscle tissue, including those involved in jaw clenches, reducing tension and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can accumulate in the body due to stress, leading to inflammation and tension in the jaw muscles, which can contribute to clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, which help reduce pain and swelling associated with jaw clenching.
Seeds: Contain a natural analgesic called sesquiterpene lactones, which provides relief from toothache-like pain caused by jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale flowers and oil of sweet almond in equal proportions weighing approximately 2 ounces each.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with the oil of sweet almond in a heatproof glass bowl heating the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool down completely then whip it with an electric mixer adding a teaspoon of beeswax until well combined taking about 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars sealing them tightly and storing them in a cool dark place for at least two weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with jaw clenching because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream reduces swelling and tension in the muscles, allowing for a relaxation of the jaw and facial muscles. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to increase blood flow to the affected area can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and bruxism. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience quick relief from jaw clenching and tension headaches, promoting a more relaxed and comfortable oral posture.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gallic acid, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw muscles and joints, alleviating tension and pain associated with clenching.
Muscle relaxants: Nettle cream contains natural muscle relaxants like flavonoids and carotenoids, which can help calm tense jaw muscles, reducing spasms and cramps caused by clenching.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in nettle cream, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and damage, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle fatigue associated with chronic clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help to soothe and calm the muscles and joints affected by jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective in relieving tension headaches caused by jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves from a reputable supplier or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250g of powdered urtica dioica with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60°c, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaw clenching are topical preparations that help alleviate the discomfort associated with this condition by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for jaw clenching contain a combination of herbs such as Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cannabis sativa, Achillea millefolium, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, jaw clenching can lead to headaches, facial pain, and difficulty sleeping, significantly impacting daily life, work productivity, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal creams are tension headaches, migraines, and TMJ disorders. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as peppermint oil, lavender oil, and arnica gel can help relax muscles and reduce pain and inflammation in these conditions. These creams may also be used to treat other conditions like arthritis, carpal tunnel syndrome, and fibromyalgia, which often involve muscle tension and stiffness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with jaw clenching is a blend of arnica and peppermint creams. Arnica cream helps to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension, while peppermint cream cools and calms the jaw muscles, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, incorporating lavender and chamomile creams can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are often underlying causes of jaw clenching. Together, these herbal creams create a soothing and effective solution for jaw clenching relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by European review for medical and pharmacological sciences, essential oil-based extracts derived from medicinal plants have been shown to exhibit antinociceptive activity, which can help reduce orofacial nociceptive behavior. The study included eight studies, with results from six experimental rodent studies showing that administration of essential oil extracts reduces orofacial nociceptive behavior, and one clinical study on children reporting that inhalation of lavender oil reduces anxiety and pain during and after tooth extraction. As a result, herbal creams containing these essential oil extracts may be beneficial for managing jaw clenching by reducing pain and anxiety associated with orofacial pain disorders.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with jaw clenching because of its relaxing properties and ability to reduce muscle tension. The herbal extract, also known as valerian root, has a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting a state of calmness and tranquility. This, in turn, helps to relax the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching episodes. Regular use of Valeriana officinalis creams may also promote better sleep quality, which can further alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps relax the muscles and reduces muscle tension, which can contribute to jaw clenching.
Valerenol: It has a sedative effect and can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are often triggers for jaw clenching.
Valeranone: It has a muscle-relaxing property and can help decrease the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are commonly used due to their high content of valerenic acid, a compound known for its muscle-relaxing and anti-anxiety properties.
Leaves: Valeriana officinalis leaves are used in creams to provide a calming effect and to help reduce muscle tension associated with jaw clenching.
Barks: The barks of Valeriana officinalis are used in creams to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve them.
Combine 500 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots with 2 liters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours and 30 minutes then strain it through cheesecloth to obtain the extract.
Mix the extract with beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain extracts from the Passiflora incarnata plant, which has a long history of use in traditional medicine to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. By calming the mind and body, these creams can help alleviate the physical tension that often contributes to jaw clenching. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata creams may also help regulate sleep patterns, which can be disrupted by jaw clenching, leading to improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle tension in the jaw and alleviate jaw clenching symptoms.
Harmane: As a potent sedative and anxiolytic, Harmane helps reduce anxiety and stress, contributing to the relaxation of jaw muscles and a decrease in jaw clenching.
Methyl flavonol glucosides: These compounds exhibit a relaxing effect on muscles and nerves, which can lead to a decrease in muscle tension and jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, leaves are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties to relieve jaw clenching pain.
Roots: Roots are used for their sedative and relaxing properties to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension in the jaw area.
Fruits: Fruits are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw muscles and joints.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup dried passiflora incarnata flowers in 2 cups boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids to obtain a herbal liquid extract.
Mix 1/4 cup of the herbal liquid extract with 1/2 cup beeswax and 1/4 cup coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, for 10-15 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with jaw clenching because of its calming properties, which can help to relax tense muscles in the jaw and face. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lavender oil may also reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bruxism, a condition characterized by habitual jaw clenching. Additionally, lavender's soothing effect on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of jaw clenching, promoting a more relaxed and peaceful state. This can help to break the cycle of jaw clenching and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to relax jaw muscles and reduce muscle tension, alleviating symptoms of jaw clenching.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce the frequency of jaw clenching.
Caryophyllene oxide: Displays anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavender leaves are used due to their calming properties, which can help relax muscles and reduce tension.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are used because of their soothing and calming effects, which can help alleviate jaw clenching and promote relaxation.
Stems: Lavender stems contain essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the body and may help reduce muscle tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers by cutting fresh blooms in the morning.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Blend the infused liquid with the wax mixture then pour it into glass jars for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain the soothing and calming properties of lemon balm, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to reduce stress and anxiety. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Melissa officinalis help to relax the jaw muscles, relieving tension and pain associated with bruxism. By applying a Melissa officinalis cream to the affected area, individuals can experience a sense of calm and relaxation, allowing their jaw muscles to release their tension and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural muscle relaxant, helping to reduce muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Limonene: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melissa officinalis leaves are often used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax tense muscles.
Roots: Melissa officinalis roots are also used for their bioactive compounds that have a calming effect, which can help reduce stress and alleviate jaw clenching.
Stems: Melissa officinalis stems contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the muscles involved in jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh melissa officinalis leaves or flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested melissa officinalis material into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors.
Mix 1 cup of chopped melissa officinalis with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain the herb oat straw, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. Oat straw is rich in avenacosides, compounds that can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw clenching. By soothing the nervous system, Avena sativa creams can help relax the jaw muscles, reducing tension and promoting relaxation. This can lead to a decrease in jaw clenching and a reduction in associated discomfort and pain. Regular use may also promote overall well-being and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Fagopyritols: These compounds help alleviate jaw clenching by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation in the facial and jaw muscles.
Fenistol: Fenistol helps to mitigate jaw clenching by acting as a natural anxiolytic and muscle relaxant, reducing stress and anxiety that contribute to teeth grinding.
Avenacosides: Avenacosides exhibit a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn helps to reduce the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching episodes.

Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and calming effects, making them suitable for relieving jaw clenching tension.
Roots: The roots of Avena sativa are used for their sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried avena sativa in a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal particles completely.
Mix the infused oil with a emulsifier such as beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 1:1:1.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to melt the beeswax and combine the ingredients smoothly.
Pour the warm mixture into a container and let it cool and thicken completely for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically, gingerol can help relax tense muscles in the jaw, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, ginger has a warming effect that can increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. This natural approach can provide relief from jaw tension and promote overall well-being. Regular use can also help prevent future episodes of jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw clenching.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene has a muscle relaxant effect, which helps to reduce muscle tension and spasms in the jaw, thus alleviating jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used in creams for jaw clenching because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort in the jaw and facial muscles.
Roots: Roots are used in creams for jaw clenching due to their analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties, which help alleviate pain and relax tense muscles.
Barks: Barks are used in creams for jaw clenching because of their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 fresh zingiber officinale roots weighing approximately 100g from a well-drained soil area.
Clean and peel the roots to remove any impurities using a vegetable brush and sharp knife.
Infuse 30g of the peeled zingiber officinale roots in 500ml of boiling water for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the cooled infused liquid to create a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the muscles. The cream's cooling effect reduces swelling and tension in the jaw, promoting relaxation and easing jaw clenching. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body, thereby reducing the urge to clench the jaw. This combination of anti-inflammatory and sedative properties makes herbal Glycyrrhiza glabra creams an effective remedy for jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Helps with jaw clenching by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and swelling in the jaw muscles.
Glycyrrhizin: Assists in reducing jaw clenching by acting as an anti-anxiety agent, thereby decreasing stress and anxiety-related jaw tension.
Licorice flavonoids: Contributes to alleviating jaw clenching by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which help protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to relax muscles and reduce inflammation.
Roots: They also contain other active compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra have a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate jaw clenching and related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 100 ml of jojoba oil and 20 ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain a unique blend of compounds, including CBD and terpenes, which work together to relax the facial muscles and reduce tension. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds help to soothe and calm the jaw and facial areas, making it easier to release clenched muscles. Additionally, the topical application of Cannabis sativa creams allows for localized relief, targeting the specific area of tension without affecting the rest of the body. This targeted approach can lead to effective pain management and relief from jaw clenching.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes help with jaw clenching by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate tension in the jaw muscles.
Cannabinoids: Cannabinoids, particularly CBD, have a relaxing effect on the muscles, which can help reduce clenching and alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jaw clenching and TMJ disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain cannabinoids like CBD, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate jaw clenching pain.
Buds: Rich in cannabinoids, including THC and CBD, they provide pain relief and relaxation, which can help reduce jaw clenching.
Seeds: They contain essential fatty acids like omega-3 and omega-6, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw clenching pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried cannabis sativa flowers and 1/4 cup of dried roots from mature plants.
Combine the dried flowers and roots in a saucepan with 1 cup of carrier oil and heat gently.
Steep the mixture at 100°f for 2 hours then strain it using cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan and heat until melted.
Combine the infused oil from step 3 with the melted beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams helps with jaw clenching because of its anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. The herb, also known as yarrow, contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce muscle spasms and tension. When applied topically, the cream can soothe and calm the muscles of the face and jaw, helping to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching. Regular use may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, contributing to a decrease in jaw clenching episodes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which may contribute to relief from jaw clenching.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and tension associated with jaw clenching.
Essential oil components (e.g. camphor, borneol): These terpenes have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help ease pain and discomfort in the jaw area caused by clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Achillea millefolium leaves are often used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and relax jaw muscles.
Flowers: The flowers of Achillea millefolium are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Stems: Achillea millefolium stems are sometimes used in creams due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and stems at dawn to preserve their potency.
Infuse 1 part of the harvested plant material in 2 parts of cold carrier oil for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix 1 part of the strained oil with 1 part of beeswax and 1 part of distilled water to create a mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 to 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with jaw clenching because they contain St. John's Wort, a natural herb known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds in Hypericum perforatum, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to reduce muscle tension and relax facial muscles, thereby alleviating the symptoms of jaw clenching. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. This can lead to improved oral health and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (mainly Hyperoside and Quercetin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation associated with jaw clenching.
Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw and face muscles, contributing to jaw clenching.
N-feruloylserotonin: This compound has a sedative effect and can help reduce muscle tension, which may be beneficial in relaxing the jaw and facial muscles that are associated with jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate jaw clenching.
Stems: Stems are used because they contain phenolic compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to soothe jaw clenching.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce jaw tension.
remedy_recipe: Collect 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Infuse 30 grams of the dried flowers in 300 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix the resulting 300 milliliters of infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture for 5-10 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy texture suitable for application.
